parsed.answer: The Denver International Airport has an automated underground baggage handling system (though it has had many problems and was eventually replaced/supplemented). There are also utility tunnels and infrastructure beneath the airport. The airport sits atop the Denver Basin, which contains natural geological formations. There are also documented underground train/shuttle tunnels connecting terminals. Conspiracy theories claim there are secret underground bunkers or government facilities, but these are unsubstantiated. The confirmed infrastructure includes the airport's own underground mechanical and utility systems, train tunnels between the main terminal and concourses, and a decommissioned automated baggage system.
